evals.expected_output: Imports a single Gong call: opens browser, extracts brief and transcript, writes to meeting file
expectations: Mode detected as Single Gong Import from gong.io/call URL
Config read and vault path verified
Session name is always -s=gong (shared session, never account-specific)
Existing session checked via tab-list before opening new browser
Playwright CLI used with --headed --persistent flags
Login check performed (snapshot or URL check for sign-in page)
Call date, title, duration, and attendees extracted from the call page
Existing meeting file matched by date and topic, or new one created via /ld-meeting
Brief extracted from Briefs tab BEFORE clicking Transcript
Video/audio elements paused immediately after page load
Transcript tab clicked and content extracted via eval
Subagent launched to write brief and transcript to meeting file
gong_url set in meeting file frontmatter
Attendees set as [[wiki-link]] format in frontmatter
Brief appended under ## External Summary following Append Rules
Transcript appended under ## Transcript following Append Rules
Browser session closed after completion
Report shows file path, attendee count, summary word count, transcript word count

evals.expected_output: Bulk imports all calls from the Gong activity page using parallel tab processing
expectations: Mode detected as Bulk Import from gong.io/account/activity URL
Activity page opened and Calls Only filter checked
gong_url saved to account file frontmatter
All call entries extracted from DOM using eval (not snapshot parsing)
Voicemail and missed calls auto-skipped
Call URLs extracted by clicking each entry and reading the Go to call link
Meeting files pre-created for all calls before starting extraction
Parallel tab pipeline used: up to 3 tabs open at once
Videos paused on every tab after loading
Each tab verified as loaded before extracting content
Briefs extracted from all tabs before switching to Transcript tabs
Transcripts extracted with wait/retry for empty content
Extra tabs closed between batches to prevent stale data
Background subagents launched for file writing while browser continues
Summary table shows all imported calls with status

evals.expected_output: Scan mode: matches existing meetings without gong_url to Gong recordings by date
expectations: Mode detected as Scan (no URL provided)
Account file read to get gong_url (activity page URL)
All meeting files listed and frontmatter checked for empty gong_url
Candidates presented in a table
Activity page opened and calls-only filter applied
Calls matched to meeting files by date
Matched calls processed and written to existing meeting files
Proceeds automatically without asking for confirmation

evals.expected_output: Imports a Granola summary into an existing or new meeting file
expectations: Mode detected as Granola Import from granola.ai URL
Browser opened with --headed flag (no --persistent needed)
Download Granola dialog dismissed if shown
Meeting title and date extracted from the page
Summary content extracted preserving markdown hierarchy
Existing meeting file matched by date, or new one created
Granola URL NOT stored in frontmatter
Summary appended under ## External Summary following Append Rules
No transcript extraction attempted (Granola shares don't include transcripts)

evals.expected_output: Handles non-recorded calls by updating attendees and gong_url only
expectations: Call page opened and no Briefs tab or Go to call button detected
Attendees still extracted from participant list
Meeting file frontmatter updated with gong_url and attendees
No brief or transcript extraction attempted
Logged as 'no recording' in the report

evals.prompt: /ld-gong Acme Corp https://app.gong.io/call?id=555 (meeting file already has Granola summary)
evals.expected_output: Appends Gong data alongside existing Granola content using subheadings, never overwrites
expectations: Import NOT skipped despite meeting file already having External Summary content from Granola
Existing Granola content in ## External Summary fully preserved
New Gong brief added under ### Gong Brief subheading within ## External Summary
Transcript added under ### Gong Transcript subheading within ## Transcript
Append Rules enforced: subheadings used when section already has content
gong_url still set in frontmatter even though file had prior content

evals.prompt: /ld-gong Acme Corp https://app.gong.io/call?id=777 (SSO login required mid-import)
evals.expected_output: Pauses for authentication, polls until user logs in, never skips the import
expectations: Page URL checked and sign-in page detected
User told to log in to the browser window
Polling every 5 seconds for up to 2 minutes to check if auth succeeded
After 2 minutes without auth, user reminded again (never gives up)
Import resumes automatically once auth succeeds
Import NEVER skipped due to auth issues

evals.prompt: /ld-gong Acme Corp (scan mode with gong_url set but External Summary empty)
evals.expected_output: Re-imports calls where gong_url was set but brief/transcript content was never extracted
expectations: Meeting files with gong_url set but empty External Summary and Transcript identified as needing import
These files included in the candidate list alongside files with empty gong_url
Existing gong_url in frontmatter does NOT cause the file to be skipped
Brief and transcript extracted and written to the file

evals.expected_output: All JavaScript eval calls use arrow function syntax and avoid regex literals
expectations: All eval commands wrapped in arrow function: '() => expr' or '() => { stmts; return val; }'
No regex literals used inside eval (no /foo/.test() patterns)
String methods like indexOf() or new RegExp() used instead of regex literals
Video/audio pausing uses querySelectorAll with for loop, not forEach
